title: Case study: derived features from transaction/health/education data
scope: Domain features in practice. Provide concrete examples of derived features across three different domains: transaction, health, and education.
key_concepts: Domain knowledge is the strongest driver for feature derivation.
Transaction data: Focusing on temporal aggregation and financial ratios.
Health data: Focusing on standard medical indices and relative changes over time.
Education data: Focusing on engagement metrics and success ratios.
examples_described: Transaction domain: Instead of raw purchase amounts, derive 'rolling 30-day average spend' or 'ratio of transaction amount to monthly income'.
Health domain: Instead of raw height and weight, derive 'Body Mass Index (BMI)' which is standard in medicine. Alternatively, 'change in blood pressure since last visit'.
Education domain: Instead of raw login counts, derive 'login streak (days in a row)' or 'ratio of passed to failed assignments'.
relations_to_other_chapters: Applies the concepts from Ch06_s01 to Ch06_s06 in concrete scenarios.

figures.title: Contoh Pembentukan Fitur Turunan di Berbagai Domain
figures.description: A table or diagram showing raw data inputs transforming into derived features across the three domains (Transaction, Health, Education).

exclude: Do not write complex SQL or pandas code for these derivations. Describe the logic conceptually.
